note: Model-free root-cause analysis for the J4 UI findings (no model calls). Explains why m-u1's /memory page rendered 0 memory cards despite the API returning 4 memories, and why 7 browser console errors fired.

finding_1_memoryView_contract_invalid.summary: GET /api/v1/memories items violate the shared `Memory` zod schema: `orgId` (required) is omitted by memoryView, and `tags` (required, z.array(z.string())) is `undefined` for tag-less extracted/manual memories.
missing_required: tags (for memories with no tags — e.g. auto-extracted)
finding_1_memoryView_contract_invalid.source: api/src/memory/resolver.ts memoryView() — returns `{ id, title, content, type, tags, tier, visibility, userId }`; `m.tags` is undefined for extracted docs and `orgId` is never included.
finding_1_memoryView_contract_invalid.consequence: The web memory store (fetchMemories -> api.memories.list) does not populate `memories`; the /memory overview tab renders 0 cards. Re-verified: 0 `.line-clamp-2` content cards on the page though the API returns 4 items.
finding_1_memoryView_contract_invalid.qa_gap: The contract suite (QA layer 3: every shared schema exercised) should reject this Memory list body but does not — a coverage gap for the memories list response.

finding_2_console_404s.summary: The /memory page fires requests to declared-but-unmounted endpoints on mount, producing browser console 404 errors (QA bar for dashboard pages = zero console errors).
failing_responses: 404 GET /api/v1/memories/stats  (memoriesEndpoints.stats declared; router only mounts GET '/:id' so it falls through to a NOT_FOUND envelope — fetchStats() hits it)
404 GET /api/v1/memories/tags   (memoriesEndpoints.listTags declared; same fall-through — fetchTags() hits it)
404 POST /api/v1/auth/refresh   (auth router mounts only login+me; Boot-A already flagged /auth/refresh unmounted — here it has a live web-side consequence)
finding_2_console_404s.note: The 7 raw console-error lines collapse to these 3 distinct failing endpoints (some fire more than once).

finding_3_extraction_parse_artifact.summary: One auto-extracted memory stored content = '**' (a stray markdown artifact) with title 'Suas preferências configuradas'; the companion memory stored correctly (title 'Empresa', content 'Padaria Central').
finding_3_extraction_parse_artifact.source: api/src/memory/extraction.ts parseFacts() line-parser mis-parsed the FAST model's markdown-bulleted output into a title with an empty/'**' content.
